title: ED sprain documentation

presenting_history.text: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact DHA sprain/strain history section supports onset, injury context, severity, functional impact, associated symptoms and relevant negatives for a sprain-compatible presentation without asserting a diagnosis.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47

red_flags.text: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag and investigation section supports clinician-recorded risk context, manual focused examination, injury severity, appearance and already performed or reviewed imaging without selecting a fracture, test or treatment.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47

red_flags.text: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.
red_flags.rationale: The exact DHA management and referral section supports recording a clinician-decided plan, specialist discussion and arranged follow-up only when those actions occurred; no management is preselected.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Muscle Sprains & Strains – 47

source_limitations: Exact joint, side, mechanism, deformity, neurovascular findings, weight bearing, diagnosis, grade, treatment, medicine, procedure and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
